content.claim: The 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks were established in 1989–1990 as part of a broad post-regime-change coalition outcome, supported by church interests (restoration of pre-WWII tradition), conservatives (return to pre-communist status quo), and liberals (preference for decentralization and local decision-making), rather than a partisan project.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Study is from 2010 and may not capture all original coalition motivations
Attribution of specific ideological drivers relies on secondary interpretation rather than primary legislative records
No direct access to the 2010 source provided in research notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
